rationales.text: The prediction is resistant to ampicillin because known resistance determinants were detected: blaSHV-2A and blaTEM-1. This is decision support, not a diagnosis; results need laboratory confirmation.

The prediction is ceftriaxone resistant because the known resistance gene blaSHV-2A was detected, which is consistent with beta-lactam resistance. This is decision support, not a diagnosis; results need laboratory confirmation.
The isolate is predicted to be resistant to tetracycline because the known resistance determinant tet(A) was detected. This is decision support only; laboratory confirmation is still needed.
The prediction is ampicillin resistant, supported by detection of the known resistance gene blaTEM-1. This is decision support only and should be confirmed by laboratory testing.

The isolate is predicted resistant to ciprofloxacin because a known resistance determinant, gyrA_D87Y, was detected. This is decision support only and should be confirmed by standard laboratory methods.
Nalidixic acid is predicted resistant, with the known resistance determinant gyrA_D87Y supporting this call. This is decision support only and should be confirmed by laboratory testing.

The isolate is predicted to be resistant to streptomycin because known resistance determinants were detected: aph(3'')-Ib and aph(6)-Id. This is decision support, not a diagnosis; results need laboratory confirmation.
The tool predicts trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole resistance, supported by detection of the known resistance gene sul2. This is decision support only and should be confirmed by laboratory testing.

The isolate is predicted to be ciprofloxacin resistant because the known resistance determinant gyrA_S83F was detected. This is decision support, not a diagnosis; results need laboratory confirmation.
The prediction is resistant to nalidixic acid because the known resistance determinant gyrA_S83F was detected. This is decision support, and the result should be confirmed by laboratory testing.
